notes.title: Organization
notes.content: HFHLLC is a Delaware LLC formed Sept 12, 2007, began operations Oct 11, 2007. Wholly owned subsidiary of Hilton Domestic Operating Company Inc. (Parent), whose equity is indirectly held by Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc. (HLT). Franchisor of Hilton family of brands in US, US territories, and Thailand for contracts executed/amended after Oct 24, 2007. Licenses IP from a Hilton affiliate on royalty-free basis.

notes.title: Basis of Presentation and Significant Accounting Policies

policies.revenue_recognition: Revenues primarily from franchise contracts with third-party hotel owners. Primary performance obligation is IP license (brand, reservations, PMS) — considered a series of distinct services transferred over time. Variable consideration via franchise royalty fees (sales-based royalties recognized as hotel room sales occur). Fixed consideration (application/initiation fees) allocated based on standalone selling prices and recognized over contract term.
policies.franchise_royalty_fees: Based on % of hotel monthly gross room revenue (and sometimes F&B/other). Billed and collected monthly. Consideration paid to incentivize hotel owners amortized over contract life as reduction to royalty fees.
policies.franchise_sales_coo_fees: Application, initiation, and other fees charged at new entry, change of ownership, or contract extension. Fixed, collected upfront, recognized over contract term.
policies.franchise_termination_fees: Recognized in period of receipt due to no further service obligation and collection uncertainty.
policies.accounts_receivable: Amounts due from franchised hotel owners. Allowance for credit losses based on historical collection, instrument nature, and forecasted conditions.
policies.financing_receivables: Loans to certain franchised hotel owners. Same allowance methodology.
policies.franchise_contracts: Contract acquisition costs (incentive payments to hotel owners) and other incremental costs capitalized. Amortized straight-line over contract term (generally 10-20 years, including extension periods at franchisor's option). Amortization begins at later of hotel opening or contract execution. Acquisition costs amortized as reduction to royalty fees; costs to obtain contracts amortized as amortization expense.
policies.impairment: Evaluate carrying value for impairment indicators. If indicators exist, compare expected undiscounted future cash flows to net carrying value. If not recoverable and exceeds fair value, recognize impairment loss. Allocate pro rata based on relative carrying values.
policies.franchise_deposits: Application fees collected upfront, recorded as current liability until non-refundable, then reclassified to deferred revenues.
policies.contract_liabilities: Non-refundable advance consideration reclassified from deposits. Recognized over contract term. Presented as deferred revenues.
policies.fair_value: Three-level hierarchy (Level 1/2/3). Financial assets and liabilities estimated equal to carrying values.
policies.acquisitions: Assess whether business combination or asset acquisition. Asset acquisitions: allocate cost (including transaction costs) to assets/liabilities based on relative fair values. No goodwill in asset acquisitions.

notes.title: Acquisition
notes.content: In May 2024, completed acquisition of Graduate franchise contracts as part of larger Hilton acquisition of Graduate brand. Accounted for as asset acquisition. Recorded franchise contract intangible assets at cost of approximately $85 million. Amortized over estimated useful life of 15 years. Activity did not have material impact on 2024 operating results.

notes.title: Fair Value Measurements
notes.content: Fair values of financial assets and liabilities estimated equal to carrying values as of Dec 31, 2024 and 2023.

notes.title: Income Taxes
notes.content: Certain US territories require taxation of franchise licensing payments to foreign-domiciled entities (includes US). Rates vary by jurisdiction. Franchisee responsible for withholding and remittance. Taxes presented as foreign tax expense. No provision for US income taxes — disregarded entity, all items included in Hilton's consolidated returns. Amounts differ between financial statements and tax returns due to accounting policy differences.

notes.title: Commitments and Contingencies
notes.content: Certain Hilton debt obligations (maturing 2025-2033) are unconditionally and irrevocably guaranteed by certain Hilton wholly owned domestic subsidiaries, including HFHLLC. All assets and franchise contracts pledged as collateral. No guarantee liability recorded due to parent-subsidiary relationship. Involved in various claims/lawsuits in ordinary course of business — ultimate resolution not expected to have material adverse effect.

notes.title: Related Party Transactions
notes.content: Intercompany balances with Hilton affiliates from centralized cash management system (not controlled by HFHLLC). Franchise deposits collected by Hilton affiliates deposited in lockbox (no HFHLLC access). Amounts due from affiliates include certain receivables repaid through periodic settlements with simultaneous distributions of retained earnings to Parent. Settled $1.5B/$1.4B/$1.55B in 2024/2023/2022 respectively. Graduate acquisition payment made by affiliate — short-term note payable recorded, settled Feb 2024. Operator agreement with Hilton affiliate — no compensation paid in any year presented.

notes.title: Subsequent Events
notes.content: Evaluated through March 18, 2024 [sic — likely March 18, 2025, matching auditor report date]. No subsequent events disclosed.
